algorithms.title: Systematic Patient Assessment Sequence
algorithms.description: The four-stage assessment framework applied to every medical patient encounter.
steps: Scene size-up: assess safety, apply standard precautions, note the nature of the illness and scene clues before contact.
Primary assessment: form a general impression, determine level of consciousness, identify and treat life threats (airway, breathing, circulation), and assign patient priority.
Secondary assessment: obtain a directed history (history dominates in medical patients), perform a focused physical exam, and obtain vital signs.
Build and refine a differential diagnosis: move from possibilities toward probabilities using the gathered information.
Reassessment: repeat the key elements on an ongoing basis, more frequently if the patient is unstable, tracking trends and treatment response.
